steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to compare tribal economic activity with two other industries across three table metrics: total contribution, employment, and average contribution per employee. The correct choice must be a claim that the table data actually supports.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'ranked highest in all three economic measures listed in the table': tribal activity does not rank highest in all three. Professional services exceeds it in total contribution ($110,157 vs. tribal's figure), and construction exceeds it in employment (77,247 vs. 51,674). ✗
  • Choice B — 'employed the same number of people as construction': tribal employment is 51,674; construction's is 77,247. These are not the same. ✗
  • Choice C — 'made the lowest total contribution by industry': construction's total contribution ($6.8 billion) is lower than tribal's ($7.3 billion), so tribal is not the lowest. ✗
  • Choice D — 'made the highest average economic contribution per employee': tribal's average of $141,510 per employee beats professional services ($110,157) and construction ($87,994). This is the one metric where tribal ranks first. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
